text: The surgeon mobilizes the liver from the diaphragm, revealing the supra-hepatic inferior vena cava. Using firefly mode, the ICG-stained liver metastasis is identified. Adhesions between the liver and the right and transverse colon are mobilized. A Pringle maneuver is applied around the hepatoduodenal ligament, and intraoperative ultrasound screens the liver for additional metastases.

The surgeon marks the resection margin on the liver surface by incising the liver capsule. The right hepatic artery is identified, clipped, and cut. Subsequently, the right hepatic branch of the portal vein is looped with a suture, clipped, and cut.

The liver is mobilized from the inferior vena cava (IVC), with selective clipping and cutting of the Spigellian veins. Parenchymal dissection is performed using a three-device technique: the assistant employs a laparoscopic aquachet, while the robotic surgeon utilizes bipolar Maryland forceps and monopolar scissors. The right bile duct is identified intrahepatically, clipped, and cut. The right hepatic vein is divided using a stapler. The liver is packed in a retrieval pack and removed through the cholecystectomy scar.
